artifacts.content:
Hey Em,

Noah finally crashed, so I’m catching up on life admin and wanted to write this out instead of just mumbling half-asleep in the morning.

Today felt like a weird mash-up of everything: I had that CBT session with Dr. Laura McKnight at Prosper Behavioral Health over lunch (week 8 already since starting on January 6), and we spent a lot of time on the panic episodes I had at the Plano office last month. I told her how I still get flashes of that near-miss on the Dallas North Tollway back on 11/03/2024 when Noah was in the back seat. She thinks that’s why I freak out if I’m in a dark, enclosed room after 5 p.m., so I’m going to ask Physical Therapy of Prosper not to put me in those back treatment rooms when I’m there in the evenings.

She also wants me to pay attention to the sertraline. It’s wild to think back to January 12, 2025, when I had that panic attack and almost blacked out driving Noah to daycare right after we’d been arguing about money. When Dr. McKenzie bumped me up to 100 mg daily at Baylor Frisco on the 20th, I didn’t realize dizziness could linger this long.

On the money front, I checked our accounts again. Between the $4,800 we still owe from Noah’s NICU stay at Children’s in Dallas (that 24‑month plan feels endless) and the leftover ACL bills, I’m relieved Physical Therapy of Prosper is in-network. I’m going to keep asking them to avoid any extra imaging or fancy add-ons unless it’s absolutely necessary.

Anyway, you’re probably already asleep at your sister’s. Text me in the morning about Noah’s drop‑off, and I’ll ice my knee after breakfast so I can actually chase him around a little.

– Michael

Running list – stuff to remember for myself (knee, money, work, etc.)

1. Knee/mental health timeline
- 10/05/2024: Blew the left ACL at Prosper Community Center playing pickup with Tyler, trying not to plow into that kid who wandered onto the court.
- 02/19/2025: Reconstruction at Baylor Scott & White Medical Center – Frisco. That’s when everything spiraled: recurrent major depressive episode with panic attacks, plus the whole oxycodone 10 mg q6h for 4 weeks situation that slid into dependence. Dr. Shah at Prosper Pain Management Clinic helped with the taper.
- Past 6 months after that surgery: basically no movement, left leg turned into a noodle, which is why PT now is so brutal.

2. Substance stuff
- DWI in Frisco on 05/18/2024 → license restricted until at least Nov 2025. Court‑ordered alcohol education at Collin County Community Supervision every other Thursday 6–8 p.m. Dad has to drive me.
- Alcohol use disorder technically in early remission. Remember the 11/02/2024 fall at Tyler’s apartment in McKinney after ~6 beers that the ortho originally linked to the ACL tear.
- Since Nov 2025: 10 mg THC edible most nights from that dispensary near Durant, Oklahoma around 11:30 p.m. → explains why 7:00 a.m. PT sessions feel like moving through molasses.

3. Work
- Two panic episodes at the Lone Star Logistics Plano office in the last month (20–30 minutes each). Grateful for the flexible schedule, but I need to keep proving I can still hit the route optimization deadlines.

4. Big picture
- Goal: full return to basketball and co‑ed soccer by late fall 2026, but only if I stay on top of rehab and don’t let the depression or money stress knock me off track again.

Subject: Can you talk this weekend?

Hey Mom,

I know we’ve chatted bits and pieces on the phone, but I wanted to put this all in one place and maybe ask if you and Dad can come up Saturday.

The knee is technically doing “okay” after the left ACL reconstruction with hamstring autograft in January, but it’s layered on top of a longer mess. Remember how Dad had that unprovoked DVT and PE at 54 while he was at the shop in Denton back in March 2021? When Dr. Desai at UT Southwestern found the same Factor V Leiden mutation in me in June 2022, she warned about long periods of immobilization or heavy compression on the leg veins (no more than 20–30 minutes at a time). I’ve been reminding the PTs about that while they’re doing manual work on my left leg.

Money is weighing on me too. We’re still on that 24‑month plan for Noah’s $4,800 NICU bill from Children’s Medical Center Dallas (May–June 2022), plus the wage garnishment Lone Star started in October 2025 for the $18,700 in medical and credit card debt from the August 2025 ACL surgery at Baylor McKinney. It feels like every time I start to catch up, another bill shows up.

On top of that, Emily and I have been doing this temporary separation since the first week of February 2026, alternating weeks with Noah between Willow Bend and her sister’s place in McKinney. When it’s my week, I’ve got to grab him from Little Explorers Preschool in Prosper by 5:45 p.m., so PT has to be earlier.

If you and Dad can swing by this weekend, maybe we can take Noah to the park and I’ll walk the flat loop while Dad wrangles him on the playground like old times.

Love,
Michael

Event: PT session – knee rehab + scheduling constraints

Date: 2026-03-04 (Wednesday)
Time: 4:00–5:00 p.m.
Location: Physical Therapy of Prosper
Attendees: Michael Thompson

Notes:
- Current phase: progressing closed‑chain strengthening, balance/proprioceptive work, blood flow restriction sessions. Still respecting post‑op precautions for left ACL reconstruction with hamstring autograft.

Medical/mental health constraints to keep in mind for myself:
- Panic/near‑syncope driving Noah to daycare on 01/12/2025 after money fight with Emily; still on sertraline 100 mg daily since Dr. McKenzie adjusted it at Baylor Scott & White – Frisco on 01/20/2025. Avoid super high‑intensity drills until I’m sure dizziness is under control.
- PTSD‑ish reaction to dark, enclosed rooms after the near‑miss car accident with Noah on the Dallas North Tollway 11/03/2024 – ask front desk not to put me in back rooms after 5 p.m.
- Factor V Leiden mutation (Dr. Desai, UT Southwestern, June 2022) + Dad’s DVT/PE history → no prolonged venous compression on left leg >20–30 minutes.

Scheduling constraints:
- License still restricted from the 05/18/2024 DWI in Frisco; Dad has to drive me if session is late‑afternoon.
- Court‑ordered alcohol education class at Collin County Community Supervision office every other Thursday 6–8 p.m. – no PT that overlaps.
- On “Noah weeks” (alternating since first week of Feb 2026 during separation with Emily), must be done by 5:30 p.m. to pick him up from Little Explorers Preschool by 5:45.

Personal goal for this visit: focus on gait quality and single‑leg control without overdoing it so I’m not wiped out for work route‑optimization project the next morning.

Subject: Need a favor with Noah + some context

Hey Jake,

Thanks again for offering to help with Noah here and there. Things have gotten more complicated than I’ve really explained over text.

You already know about the left ACL reconstruction and all the PT, but there’s a whole tangle around it. Remember that blackout at The Brass Tap in Frisco on 11/09/2024 when we were out and I ended up at Medical City Plano with a mild concussion? In December, Dr. Collins told me I needed to stay away from anything in PT that could increase fall risk, especially balance work on unstable surfaces, until I’ve gone at least 6 months without another alcohol‑related blackout. That’s part of why I’m so cautious about the rehab.

Then there’s the CPS angle. In early January 2025, a neighbor reported that I left Noah in the waiting room too long during an appointment at Lone Star Pediatrics in Frisco. CPS opened an investigation, and my caseworker, Danielle Ortiz, told me not to leave him unsupervised in medical offices. So when I have PT at Physical Therapy of Prosper on Monday and Wednesday afternoons, I really can’t drag Noah along and park him in the lobby.

Layer on top of that the DWI from 05/18/2024 in Frisco – my license is restricted until at least November 2025, and I’ve got that court‑ordered alcohol education program at the Collin County Community Supervision office every other Thursday from 6–8 p.m. Dad’s already doing a lot of driving.

So… when you say you can watch Noah on some Mon/Wed afternoons, it’s honestly a huge deal. It lets me keep rehabbing the knee without risking more CPS drama or blowing off the court stuff.

I owe you one (or ten).

Mike

metadata.subject: Need a favor with Noah + some context

artifacts.content:
Journal – late night brain dump

It’s 11:45 p.m., Emily’s at her sister’s in McKinney (our “off” week with Noah), and I just took my usual 10 mg THC edible from that dispensary outside Durant. I know I started doing this in November 2025 to cope with those late‑night data pulls for Lone Star, but it’s catching up with me. No wonder I feel half‑asleep at 7:00 a.m. PT sessions.

I keep replaying all the different ways my choices have messed with this knee. There was that stretch between March and August 2024 at the Lone Star Logistics Plano office when Daniel had me sprinting three flights of stairs 10–12 times a day to chase dashboard deadlines. That’s when the chronic left knee pain and stiffness really started. Then the alcohol stuff: the DWI in Frisco on 05/18/2024, the blackout at Brass Tap on 11/09/2024, the fall at Tyler’s apartment in McKinney on 11/02/2024 after about six beers. No surprise someone wrote “alcohol use disorder in early remission” in my chart before the 02/19/2025 surgery at Baylor Frisco.

And it’s not just booze. After that ACL reconstruction, they had me on oxycodone 10 mg every 6 hours for four weeks. Dr. Shah at Prosper Pain Management was right to call it opioid dependence and walk me through the taper, but I still feel guilty about how foggy I was with Noah during that time.

I want the next chapter to look different: stick with CBT at Prosper Behavioral Health, keep alcohol out of the picture, cut down the edibles, and get this knee strong enough that I can run around with Noah without worrying about passing out, falling, or ending up in another ER.

Subject: Quick life update (knee, work, court stuff)

Tyler,

Figured I’d send an actual update instead of just replying with memes in the group chat.

Knee: Still grinding away at Physical Therapy of Prosper. Hard to believe it all started with that 90‑minute pickup game at the Prosper Community Center on 10/05/2024 when I twisted my left knee trying not to truck that kid who wandered onto the court with us. Hamstring autograft, months of stiffness, quad weakness – you know the drill.

Mental health: I’m in an 8‑week CBT program with Dr. McKnight at Prosper Behavioral Health (started January 6). I told her about the two panic episodes at the Lone Star office in Plano last month – each 20–30 minutes where my heart was racing and I thought I was going to pass out in the conference room. She’s helping me figure out how to not completely melt down every time my knee twinges.

Work: Still on the big route‑optimization project. Thankfully they gave me a flexible schedule after the panic stuff, but I’m trying not to abuse it, especially with my history. HR still has the DWI from 05/18/2024 in Frisco and the performance‑improvement plan from last year in my file, so I’m walking a fine line.

Home: Emily and I started a temporary separation the first week of February 2026. We’re alternating weeks with Noah between the Willow Bend house and her sister’s apartment in McKinney. Mediation is set for March 14, 2026 at the Collin County Courthouse. On my weeks, I can’t do PT after 5:30 because I have to grab him from Little Explorers Preschool by 5:45.

I still want the long‑term goal to be getting back to pickup and co‑ed soccer with you by late fall 2026. Just might be a slower comeback than we pictured.

– Mike

metadata.subject: Quick life update (knee, work, court stuff)

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Aaron Thompson <m.thompson@gmail.com>
To: Emily Grace Thompson <emily.thompson@example.com>
Subject: PT schedule, money stuff, and all the other landmines
Date: 2026-03-03

Em,

I’m trying to pull everything together before I go back into Physical Therapy of Prosper next week, and it’s a lot.

On the money side, we’re still digging out from the $6,800 we owe for the 10/09/2024 ACL reconstruction at Baylor Scott & White Medical Center–Frisco, plus being two months behind with Prosper State Bank on the mortgage back in November and December 2024. On top of that, we’ve still got the $2,300 balance from your C‑section at Medical City Plano. Because of all that, I’m going to ask them to keep my post‑op ACL rehab to twice a week for the 8‑week authorization window from Lone Star Logistics Group’s disability/insurance, and then drop to once a week after the initial 4 weeks unless the surgeon says it’s absolutely necessary.

I also have to work around Lone Star’s drama. After missing those 3 days in October 2024 for pre‑op testing at Texas Health Presbyterian Hospital Plano, Sarah Nguyen put that written warning in my file on November 1, 2024 and told me I have to stay able to work at my desk at least 7 hours a day, Monday through Friday, or I could lose my job. And after Noah’s hospital stay, HR hit me with a final written warning in that 11/07/2024 meeting at the Dallas office about “attendance issues,” so I’m going to tell PT I can only do after 5:30 pm on Mondays and Wednesdays.

I know this is a lot, but I’m really trying to take the rehab seriously this time and still keep us afloat.

– Michael

metadata.subject: PT schedule, money stuff, and all the other landmines

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Aaron Thompson <m.thompson@gmail.com>
To: Dr. Patel <dr.patel@primarycareclinic.com>
Subject: Context and constraints for updated ACL rehab plan
Date: 2026-03-04

Hi Dr. Patel,

Ahead of my follow‑up, I wanted to give you some context about this left ACL situation and the post‑operative rehab protocol so we can set realistic guidelines.

This is technically my second major left knee injury in three years. I never followed through on the preventive PT you recommended back in May 2022, when the chronic instability was already making it harder to lift Noah and help Emily around the house at 1483 Willow Bend Drive. Since then, I’ve had multiple incidents: the weekend recreational basketball injury on August 3, 2024 at the Prosper Community Recreation Center playing with Tyler Carter (I landed awkwardly after another player undercut me, briefly blacked out, and had to go by ambulance to Baylor Scott & White Medical Center – McKinney), the fall in August 2024 when I slipped carrying Noah down the stairs at home after taking one of Emily’s leftover hydrocodone tablets, which led to an ER incident report at Baylor McKinney and a CPS home visit on August 20, 2024, and then the re‑injury in the Lone Star Logistics Group corporate basketball tournament at Frisco Fieldhouse on July 27, 2024, just two weeks after HR put me on a performance‑improvement plan for missed deadlines due to earlier PT appointments.

All of that culminated in the left ACL reconstruction with hamstring autograft you have records for from September 9, 2024 at Baylor Scott & White Medical Center – McKinney. Given this history, I’d appreciate your input on a rehab protocol that’s effective but doesn’t set me up for more instability or work issues.

Best,
Michael

metadata.subject: Context and constraints for updated ACL rehab plan

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Aaron Thompson <m.thompson@gmail.com>
To: Physical Therapy of Prosper Intake <info@ptofprosper.com>
Subject: Scheduling + special precautions for ACL rehab
Date: 2026-03-05

Hello,

I’m confirming my evaluation for post‑operative rehab of my left ACL reconstruction and wanted to outline some special instructions and legal/work constraints you’ll need to factor into the plan.

1) Custody/parenting: Because of the custody clause in the temporary separation agreement my wife, Emily Grace Thompson, and I signed on February 5, 2025 at the Collin County Family Court in McKinney, I am not allowed to lift Noah or carry him up or down the stairs at our Prosper townhouse unless my left knee is braced. During my Tuesday and Thursday evening visitation, I must be able to safely manage 10–15 minutes of carrying Noah, so please avoid exercises that leave the knee too unstable or painful for that.

2) Legal obligations: Please do not schedule any sessions on Thursday evenings after 6 p.m. I have a court‑ordered DWI education class at the Collin County Community Supervision office in McKinney related to my September 14, 2023 arrest, and missing it could affect my probation.

3) Prior injuries/medical history: I have metal hardware from a right femur surgery at Baylor Scott & White Medical Center in Frisco on 06/03/2019, so please avoid modalities that could interfere with that area. Also, ever since a forklift accident at Lone Star Logistics Group’s Plano warehouse on 02/19/2025, when I was stuck under a pallet for about 20 minutes, I’ve had panic attacks with pressure around my neck and upper chest—please avoid manual therapy there.

4) Privacy: If my brother, Jacob Daniel Thompson, contacts you, please do not discuss the 2019 femur surgery, my failed DOT physical in March 2023, or the lost promotion to regional logistics lead.

Thanks for working within these constraints.

Best,
Michael Aaron Thompson
